title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Kensington, Brooklyn | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Kensington, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Kensington's charming pre-war homes and historic apartment buildings can unfortunately provide ideal roosting spots for bats seeking shelter in Brooklyn. Our professional bat removal and exclusion services protect Kensington residents from health risks and property damage caused by bat infestations. Best Pest Control NYC offers safe, humane, and effective bat control solutions tailored to the unique architecture of this beloved Brooklyn neighborhood.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over 15 years of experience serving Kensington and surrounding Brooklyn communities with licensed, insured bat removal specialists who understand local building codes and wildlife regulations. We use proven exclusion techniques that permanently solve your bat problem while ensuring the safety of your family and compliance with all NYC wildlife protection laws.
localContext: Kensington's tree-lined streets, proximity to Prospect Park, and abundance of early 20th-century brick and brownstone buildings create an environment where bats often find entry through deteriorating roof lines, loose soffits, and attic vents. The neighborhood's mix of single-family homes near Coney Island Avenue and multi-family dwellings closer to Ocean Parkway all require specialized bat exclusion approaches that preserve architectural integrity while eliminating bat access points.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Kensington, Brooklyn?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Kensington typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in NYC?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bats are protected wildlife in New York, so removal must be done humanely through exclusion methods rather than extermination. Our licensed professionals follow all NYC and state regulations, using one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, typically performed outside of maternity season (May-August) to avoid trapping flightless young.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my Kensington home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, dark staining around potential entry points, accumulation of guano (bat droppings) in attics or along exterior walls, and seeing bats entering or exiting your home at twilight. You may also notice a strong ammonia-like odor from urine and droppings in affected areas.

faqs.question: How long does the bat exclusion process take?
faqs.answer: The complete bat exclusion process typically takes 2-4 weeks in Kensington homes. After the initial inspection and sealing of entry points with one-way devices, we monitor for 1-2 weeks to ensure all bats have exited, then permanently seal all access points. Follow-up visits and attic cleanup or sanitization may add additional time depending on the severity of the infestation.
